nativeRange.habitat: Damp ground in fields, lawns, sidewalk cracks, and along stream and pond margins; tolerates occasional submersion and grows readily as a creeping marginal plant

decorPreferences: strong overhead lighting for compact carpeting

avoidWith: plant-eating fish such as goldfish, large cichlids, or silver dollars

summary: Hydrocotyle sibthorpioides (Lawn Marshpennywort, sometimes sold as 'Pennywort Mini') is a small Asian creeping plant with round, kidney-shaped leaves. It is generally used as a foreground carpet and tends to stay compact under strong light with added CO2.
careNotes: Hydrocotyle sibthorpioides is a small creeping plant native to southeastern Asia, recognizable by its tiny round-to-kidney-shaped leaves (typically 0.5-2 cm across) with shallowly scalloped edges. It is distinct from its sister species H. verticillata (which has larger half-dollar leaves arranged in whorls), H. leucocephala (Brazilian Pennywort, a stem plant with larger round leaves), and H. tripartita (the Japan/Mini form with three-lobed clover-like leaves). It is most often used as a foreground or low midground carpet and propagates by sending out runners. Submerged height is generally reported in the 1-10 cm range; the larger 'up to 5-10 inches' figures cited by some retailers refer to emersed or untrimmed growth. Strong lighting is generally advised to keep the plant low and compact - in medium light without CO2 it tends to grow taller, more leggy, and slower. CO2 injection (commonly cited around 20-30 mg/L) along with a nutrient-rich substrate and regular dosing of nitrate, phosphate, iron, and trace micronutrients is the typical setup recommended for a successful carpet. Without CO2 it can still survive in medium light but is reported to grow much more slowly and is less likely to form a dense carpet. Temperature tolerance is wide (sources report 10-30 C survival); the values written here use the commonly recommended aquarium range of around 20-28 C. Cross-source notes: pH was given as 5-7 by Fish Laboratory (citing the broader botanical range) and 6.5-7.5 by Buce Plant; a combined 5.5-7.5 range is recorded here. Care level was rated 'easy to moderate' by Buce Plant and Fish Laboratory but 'medium' by Aquasabi, with a strong note that without CO2 and high light the plant struggles - 'intermediate' is used as the cross-source middle value. Lighting was given as 'medium' by Buce Plant and Fish Laboratory but 'high' by Aquasabi; 'high' is selected because all sources agree that the compact carpeting form is only achieved under strong light. Growth rate was described as 'moderate to fast' by retail sources and 'medium' by Aquasabi - 'medium' is recorded as the conservative average. Family was given as Araliaceae across all modern sources (APG taxonomy), replacing the older Apiaceae placement. Minimum tank volume of 40 L is a conservative floor for a small planted setup and is not directly stated by any source. The 'Pennywort Mini' trade name is sometimes used; care should be taken not to confuse it with Hydrocotyle tripartita 'Mini'. Primary source note: the manifest-supplied Buce Plant URL (the 'Variegata' product page) returned 404 at access time; the standard tissue-culture product pages on Buce Plant for the non-variegated form were used in its place, and the swap is documented in sources.additional below.
breedingNotes: Propagation is straightforward via vegetative means: cut a section of the creeping stem and replant the segment, separate runners or daughter plants from a healthy mother plant, or pull apart and split dense clumps. New shoots can be trimmed off anywhere along a stem and replanted to thicken the carpet.

primary.name: Buce Plant
primary.notes: Manifest-supplied URL for the 'Variegata' product page returned 404; this UNS tissue-culture product page for the non-variegated form on the same retailer was used as the primary source instead.

additional.name: Wikipedia
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (Araliaceae / Apiales), native range (southeastern Asia), leaf morphology (kidney-shaped to round, 0.5-2 cm), and broad temperature tolerance (10-30 C, optimum 20-28 C).

additional.name: Fish Laboratory
additional.notes: Cross-checked pH (5-7), temperature (10-30 C, optimum 20-28 C), lighting (medium), CO2 needs (20-30 mg/L), growth rate, propagation, and care level.

additional.name: Aquasabi
additional.notes: Cross-checked submerged height (1-10 cm), temperature (22-28 C ideal), lighting (high), CO2 (recommended), difficulty (medium), and growth rate (medium). Notes water parameters are of minor importance for this species.
